title: FFI save_identity_to_file: non-atomic write corrupts keyfile on crash
description: save_identity_to_file uses fs::write() which is not atomic: it truncates then writes. If killed mid-write, the identity file is left with partial content. On next launch, load_identity_from_file returns Err('identity file is N bytes, expected 64') and the app has no identity. Trigger: OOM-kill or SIGKILL during first identity save. Fix: write to temp file then rename (same pattern as SentIds::persist_to_file).
